question_text: Name one way to decrease the pressure exerted by a solid object on a surface.
correct_answer: Increase the surface area in contact with the surface (or reduce the force applied).
explanation: Pressure is calculated using the formula: pressure = force ÷ area. To decrease pressure, you can either increase the contact area (spreading the force over a larger surface) or reduce the force being applied. A real-world example is snow shoes, which have a large surface area to distribute your weight, reducing the pressure on the snow so you don't sink.
